summary: SKBitmap.Decode(codec, imageInfo) returns null when the target imageInfo specifies SKColorType.Gray8, a regression introduced in 1.68.0 where decoding grayscale PNGs to a 1-byte-per-pixel format no longer works.

bugSignals.errorMessage: bitmap == null when using SKBitmap.Decode(codec, imageInfo) with SKColorType.Gray8

stepsToReproduce: Read a grayscale PNG file as bytes
Create SKCodec from MemoryStream of the bytes
Build SKImageInfo with codec dimensions and SKColorType.Gray8
Observe that the returned bitmap is null
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp 1.68.0, Visual Studio Code, netcoreapp3.0. Confirmed still broken in 3.119.1 (Jan 2026).

repoLinks.description: Maintainer comment on #694 suggesting the codec-based Gray8 decode pattern — same pattern now broken

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: User confirmed still broken in 3.119.1 as of January 2026. No fix or related PR has been identified.

regression.reason: Reporter explicitly states 1.60.3 worked (BytesPerPixel==1 returned naturally), 1.68.0 broke it. Additionally the explicit Gray8 override suggested in #694 also returns null in 1.68.0+.

analysis.summary: SKBitmap.Decode(SKCodec, SKImageInfo) with Gray8 target returns null because the native sk_codec_get_pixels call returns a non-success error code when asked to decode to SKColorType.Gray8, causing the C# wrapper to dispose the bitmap and return null. This is a regression: 1.60.3 automatically returned a Gray8 bitmap for grayscale PNGs, but 1.68.0 changed the default to RGBA and also broke the explicit Gray8 override path suggested in #694.
analysis.rationale: The bug is clearly in SKBitmap.Decode(SKCodec, SKImageInfo) at lines 446-459 of SKBitmap.cs. The check at line 454 disposes and nulls the bitmap if GetPixels returns anything other than Success or IncompleteInput. When the target format is Gray8, the native Skia codec likely returns an error (InvalidConversion) for format conversion that was previously handled differently. This is confirmed as a regression by the reporter and acknowledged by the maintainer (mattleibow) who said they reproduce the null result. Confirmed open in 3.119.1.

keySignals.text: In 1.60.3 loading an 8bit png would result in a bitmap with BytesPerPixel == 1. In 1.68.0, BytesPerPixel == 4
keySignals.interpretation: Default decoding behavior changed between versions — Gray8 PNGs are now decoded to RGBA by default.

keySignals.text: var bitmap = SKBitmap.Decode(codec, imageInfo); // bitmap == null
keySignals.interpretation: Explicit Gray8 override via codec also fails, meaning the workaround from #694 is now broken too.

keySignals.text: I also get the null result when I believe it should be working.
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer confirmed the null result, indicating this is a genuine bug not a usage error.

keySignals.text: This issue still persists in version 3.119.1.
keySignals.interpretation: Not fixed in recent releases — still an active open issue.

codeInvestigation.finding: Decode(SKCodec, SKImageInfo) creates a bitmap with the requested info, calls codec.GetPixels(), and returns null if result != Success and result != IncompleteInput. When bitmapInfo uses Gray8, the native codec returns an error and bitmap is disposed and nulled.

GetPixels(SKImageInfo, IntPtr, int, SKCodecOptions) delegates directly to native sk_codec_get_pixels with the target format. No Gray8 special-casing exists — the format is passed through to native Skia which may reject the conversion.

The Decode(SKCodec) overload uses codec.Info directly (which would give the native pixel format for the image). For grayscale PNGs this may have changed from Gray8 to RGBA between 1.60.3 and 1.68.0, consistent with reporter observation.

nextQuestions: What SKCodecResult value is returned by GetPixels when target is Gray8? (InvalidConversion? Unimplemented?)
Does sk_codec_get_pixels support Gray8 as target format in any supported Skia version?
Would decoding to RGBA then CopyTo(Gray8) work as a workaround?

resolution.hypothesis: The Skia native codec does not support direct decode to Gray8 format via sk_codec_get_pixels in 1.68.0+. The SkiaSharp wrapper should either: (a) implement a decode-then-convert path when the target format is Gray8, or (b) detect the unsupported conversion and return a better error.

proposals.title: Decode to native format then convert to Gray8
proposals.description: As a workaround, decode to the codec's native format (likely RGBA), then use SKBitmap.CopyTo to convert to Gray8. This avoids the failing native codec conversion path.

proposals.title: Fix SKBitmap.Decode to fall back to decode-then-convert for unsupported target formats
proposals.description: In Decode(SKCodec, SKImageInfo), after getting a non-success result, check if the failure is due to unsupported format and attempt a decode-then-convert fallback using the codec's native format followed by CopyTo.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Decode to native format then convert to Gray8
resolution.recommendedReason: Users can apply this workaround immediately without waiting for a fix. The fix proposal needs deeper investigation into why the codec rejects Gray8 before implementation.

actionability.reason: Bug is confirmed by maintainer and persists in 3.119.1. Root cause is in SKBitmap.Decode interaction with native codec for Gray8 target. Repro code and input image provided. Investigation needed to determine exact error code returned and best fix path.

actions.description: Apply bug, core API, and tenet labels
